title: Workbench Per-Page-Type Design Research
label: workbench-per-page-design
purpose: Before any more Workbench shell implementation work, do real per-page-type design research and produce mockups for every distinct page type the platform needs. The current trajectory was building a website-style chrome with empty rooms; the user re-framed FAMtastic as a creation platform that needs canvas-dominant layouts with collapsible glass chrome, distinct layouts per page type, and per-domain icon sets.
why_now: User feedback session 2026-05-05 (cap_creation-product-design-direction-2026-05-05): the prior implementation defaulted to one-size-fits-all chrome and never asked 'what kind of page is this and what layout does it need?' The 5 mockups in docs/mockups/ are a partial start; they cover home, page editor, components, media generation, Shay trace — but they're inconsistent and incomplete for the page-type taxonomy the user described (Library, Creation Canvas, Workshop/Sandbox, Triage, Settings, Editor-with-Chat).

origin.trigger_record: Session 2026-05-05 — Workbench shell reconciliation + design recovery
origin.summary: User saw the 3-pane shell-compare viewer, recognized the design intent was preserved in mockups but lost in implementation, and provided a long architectural reframe distinguishing creation-platform from website. They named six distinct page types and called out gaps in research per type.

success_signal: (1) Six page-type design briefs land in docs/design-research/page-types/, each citing at least 2 product references and proposing a layout pattern. (2) Per-domain icon-set briefs land for at least Sites, Media, Components. (3) New mockup HTMLs land in docs/mockups/ extending the existing 5 to cover the full page-type taxonomy. (4) The Workbench Foundation implementation plan is paused until these briefs land, formalized via a needs_tasking closeout on plan_2026_05_05_ops_workspace_gui pointing here.
non_goals: Implementing any of the new mockups (separate plan after research is approved)
Choosing between Path A / B / C from the shell-reconciliation doc (still deferred)
Drawing actual final icons (icon-set BRIEFS only — visual style + reference grid; final icon production happens after a tool/illustrator decision)

page_types_in_scope.name: Library / Collection
needs: optional operational widgets area on a domain home

page_types_in_scope.name: Creation Canvas
needs: Shay-Shay has a defined location near the canvas

page_types_in_scope.name: Triage / Workshop (Shay-Shay)
applies_to: Shay-Shay's own workshop page where bigger tasks land

page_types_in_scope.name: Editor with Chat
needs: two distinct chats: Studio chat (per-page work) vs Shay-Shay (cross-cutting) — relationship and surfaces designed

workstreams.title: Page-type taxonomy document
outputs: docs/design-research/page-type-taxonomy.md — the canonical list of 6 page types, what each is for, what layout pattern it follows, where it appears in the platform
acceptance: Every existing surface in both shells (index.html + workbench-foundation.html) maps to exactly one page-type.
No page is left without a type.

workstreams.title: Library/Collection layout research
outputs: docs/design-research/page-types/01-library-collection.md
Cite at least 2 product references (e.g. Linear backlog, Figma file browser, Apple Photos library, Notion gallery, Codex Desktop project picker)
Mockup screenshot or sketch in docs/design-research/page-types/01-library-collection-mockup.html
Concrete layout: where search lives, where filters live, what card looks like, where operational-widgets area lives if applicable

workstreams.title: Creation Canvas layout research (the most important one)
outputs: docs/design-research/page-types/02-creation-canvas.md
Reference: Cursor, Leonardo AI, Midjourney, Figma, Pets-assistant pattern user shared
How chrome collapses when user is creating: which keys, which gestures, what's the default state
Where Shay-Shay lives so she can see canvas + proofs simultaneously
Right rail glass slide-out spec: when does it open, what triggers, animation pattern, sections inside
Mockup: docs/design-research/page-types/02-creation-canvas-mockup.html

workstreams.title: Workshop/Sandbox layout research
outputs: docs/design-research/page-types/03-workshop-sandbox.md
Reference: JSFiddle, CodePen, StackBlitz, Storybook
Multi-pane layout spec, runner controls, state inspector pattern
Mockup: docs/design-research/page-types/03-workshop-sandbox-mockup.html

workstreams.title: Shay-Shay's Workshop / Triage page research
outputs: docs/design-research/page-types/04-shay-workshop.md
Reference: Linear inbox + agent dashboards (LangGraph, CrewAI), Cursor agent panel
How Shay's workshop page differs from contextual Shay (per-page assistant vs. her own workspace)
Mockup: docs/design-research/page-types/04-shay-workshop-mockup.html

workstreams.title: Settings / Customization page research
outputs: docs/design-research/page-types/05-settings.md — short, mostly conventional
Reference: Linear settings, Notion settings, Apple System Settings
Mockup: docs/design-research/page-types/05-settings-mockup.html

workstreams.title: Editor-with-chat layout research
outputs: docs/design-research/page-types/06-editor-with-chat.md
Reference: Cursor (chat sidebar pattern), Notion AI, Codex Desktop chat pane
The Studio-chat vs Shay-Shay relationship — when does which surface speak, do they coexist, is there a handoff
Mockup: docs/design-research/page-types/06-editor-with-chat-mockup.html

workstreams.title: Chrome collapse + glass slide-out pattern research (cross-cutting)
outputs: docs/design-research/cross-cutting/01-chrome-collapse.md — when chrome collapses, what triggers it, animation timing, accessibility
docs/design-research/cross-cutting/02-glass-slide-out.md — the canonical glass panel slide-out spec (right rail, secondary nav, modal)
Reference: macOS sidebars, Cursor command palette, Cassidy Williams' floating glass nav patterns, Aceternity/Cult UI glass components
CSS prototype HTML: docs/design-research/cross-cutting/glass-slide-out-prototype.html

workstreams.title: Per-domain icon set briefs
outputs: docs/design-research/icon-sets/sites.md — visual direction (cards, grids, globes, portfolio metaphors), reference set, candidate symbol library
docs/design-research/icon-sets/brainstorm.md
docs/design-research/icon-sets/plans.md
docs/design-research/icon-sets/components.md (cubes, brackets, code metaphors)
docs/design-research/icon-sets/media.md (sparkles, image, video, generation metaphors)
docs/design-research/icon-sets/research.md
docs/design-research/icon-sets/admin.md
Each brief: 2+ reference icon sets (Phosphor, Lucide, Tabler, Iconoir, Heroicons, custom-illustrated), recommended set, hover state spec

workstreams.title: Consolidate research into a final shell-rebuild plan
outputs: A new plan plan_2026_05_XX_workbench_shell_rebuild that uses the research deliverables to scope an actual implementation
The new plan picks Path A / B / C from the shell-reconciliation doc with the user's input
Closeout for plan_2026_05_05_ops_workspace_gui marking it superseded-by-or-pending-rebuild

scope: ws_research_creation_canvas (the most important — this is the page type the platform actually creates value on)
ws_research_chrome_glass_pattern (cross-cutting prerequisite for every page-type design)
mvp.rationale: These three unblock every other research deliverable. Once the taxonomy and the canvas + chrome patterns are settled, the other page types are variations on a theme. Library / Workshop / Settings / Editor-with-chat all reuse the chrome rules.
deferred: ws_consolidation_plan (P0, but blocked until others land)

decisions_confirmed.summary: FAMtastic is a creation platform, not a website. Every layout decision flows from this.
Different page types get different layouts. Six types identified.
Chrome collapses when user is actively creating; canvas dominates.
Right rail and secondary nav are glass slide-outs, not stuck panels.
Each domain gets its own coherent icon set (not generic Lucide soup).
Pause Workbench implementation until per-page-type design research is done.

known_gaps_opened: No existing taxonomy doc maps surfaces → page types.
Cross-cutting glass slide-out CSS pattern doesn't exist yet — only static glass panels in workbench-foundation.css.
No per-domain icon set briefs exist; current rail uses single-letter text glyphs.
The Studio-chat vs Shay-Shay surface relationship is undefined.
